title: What is an apostle?
body:
.# OVERVIEW


## scripture
- is the panim Yah the first apostle Gen 3.8?
- flaming cherubim messengers?
- Joseph being sent going ahead into the land of Egypt to prepare local land meeting joining with Yahweh and people
- Moshe at Sinai at 80 burning bush/bush on fire

## questions

1. What is the crux of apostleship as a concept in the Torah?
2. facilitating a local meeting or joining with Yahweh in land
3. Connected to being a messenger With the idea that the messenger is deputized as a representative of the king or authority that sent the messenger an extension of the king's authority to order and prepare the people that they are sent to for a local meeting or joining with God
4. This seems very connected to Priestly duty priesthood

comments.author: joshualinog
comments.body:
G652

Original: ἀπόστολος

Transliteration: apostolos

Phonetic: ap-os'-tol-os

Thayer Definition:

1. a delegate, messenger, one sent forth with orders
   1. specifically applied to the twelve apostles of Christ
   2. in a broader sense applied to other eminent Christian teachers
      1. of Barnabas
      2. of Timothy and Silvanus

Origin: from G649

TDNT entry: 07:47,7

Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

Strong's Definition: From G649; a delegate ; specifically an ambassador of the Gospel; officially a commissioner of Christ (" apostle" ), (with miraculous powers): - apostle, messenger, he that is sent.

Total KJV Occurrences: 81

apostles (15)
Mat 10:2; Luk 6:13; Luk 11:49; Luk 22:14; Act 1:26; 1Co 9:5; 1Co 12:28; 1Co 12:29; 2Co 11:5; 2Co 12:11; Eph 3:5; Eph 4:11; Rev 2:2; Rev 18:20; Rev 21:14 (refs15)
